documentation.name: 00-conventions.md
documentation.summary: Documentation authoring conventions for the docs/ tree: directory layout, file naming rules, the mandatory header block (version | date | status), version-bump procedure, cross-reference/path rules, and the requirement to keep README.md in sync. Must be read before editing any docs/ file.

documentation.name: 01-research.md
documentation.summary: Foundational architecture research comparing three reference projects (MiroFish, Stanford Generative Agents/Reverie, ai-town) and proposing the political simulation's core mechanics: action sets, an institutional rules engine, a layered engine architecture, a dual-tier public/hidden stance model with a pressure function, and PoC validation scenarios.

documentation.name: 02-gaps.md
documentation.summary: Gap analysis enumerating 18 design and engineering shortfalls found in 01-research.md, each marked resolved or unresolved with a link to the design doc that closes it. The project's single backlog index.

documentation.name: 03-project-references.md
documentation.summary: Technical index mapping specific modules of three local reference codebases to the unresolved gaps in 02-gaps.md, with concrete code-reuse guidance.

documentation.name: README.md
documentation.summary: Documentation index defining the recommended reading order, a directory map of the docs/ tree, and a quick-reference table cross-checking each of the 18 gaps against its resolving design doc.

documentation.name: bill-model.md
documentation.summary: Pins down the bill data schema: structured articles with topic tags, a stanceVector projection, a stage/committee workflow, an amendment version chain, and session-bound expiry.

documentation.name: external-event-model.md
documentation.summary: Defines two separate external-injection channels — Admin Override and World Event — with a shared, auditable externalEvents schema.

documentation.name: llm-cost-model.md
documentation.summary: Classifies LLM calls into A/B/C tiers, a dot-product shortcut for decideVote, a three-layer Reflect throttle, prompt-caching strategy, and per-scale cost estimates with a budget-cap degradation ladder.

documentation.name: media-model.md
documentation.summary: Specifies a three-channel opinion model (Media, Pollster, Campaign), sentiment/confidence update formulas, a rule-based media_tick scoring/framing algorithm, and the media_spotlight decay formula feeding the pressure function.

documentation.name: observability-model.md
documentation.summary: Defines three observability schemas (llmCallLog, tickSnapshot, stanceDriftLog) plus PoC acceptance metrics and budget-alerting logic.

documentation.name: politician-skill-contract.md
documentation.summary: Locks the field-mapping contract from the external Politician-skill repo's outputs into the simulation Agent schema, including backbone-derivation, hidden.social estimation, and Zep relationship-graph seeding.

documentation.name: product-positioning.md
documentation.summary: States the dual-audience product positioning (researchers vs. game developers) and derives a two-layer interface split, plus an ethics disclaimer policy.

documentation.name: time-model.md
documentation.summary: Defines the three-layer time hierarchy (tick/session/cycle) with event-driven session closure and a per-topic exponential stance-decay formula.

documentation.name: design-priority-three.md
documentation.summary: Superseded v1 three-in-one design proposal covering the bill data schema, the three-tier time/session clock model, and the media/public-opinion layer; archived after being split into standalone v2 design docs.

list.name: Governance & Navigation
list.description: The authoring rulebook and the documentation index that together govern how the docs/ tree is structured, named, versioned, and navigated.

list.name: Research & Backlog
list.description: The foundational architecture research, its derived gap-analysis backlog, and the reference-codebase index that maps unresolved gaps to reusable implementation patterns.

list.name: Subsystem Design
list.description: The current, pinned v2 design specifications for each simulation subsystem, each closing one or more gaps from the backlog.

list.name: Archived Designs
list.description: Superseded design proposals kept for historical traceability after their content was split and replaced by standalone v2 documents.
